steps.title: Identify the noun and its identifying title
steps.content: The left side of the blank contains the noun “song” (in “the hit song”). The right side begins with a title in quotation marks, “Little Marie,” that directly identifies the song.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for titles
steps.content:
When a noun is immediately followed by a title, no punctuation separates them.

Choice A correctly keeps the noun and its title together without punctuation.

The other options incorrectly insert punctuation between a noun and its identifying title.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
